body:
Bumps [redis](https://github.com/redis/node-redis) from 4.7.1 to 6.0.0.
Release notes

Sourced from redis's releases.

redis@6.0.0

Changes

This is the first major release of node-redis since 5.x. The headline change is RESP3 by default, alongside broader Redis 8.8 command coverage, pubsub/cluster reliability fixes, and a bumped minimum Node.js version.

Key changes

  • RESP3 is the default protocol — opt back into RESP2 explicitly if you need the old wire format
  • Node.js 20 is the new minimum supported runtime
  • Redis 8.8 coverage — new array commands, INCREX / INCREXBYFLOAT, ZINTER/ZUNION COUNT aggregator, XNACK, CLIENT UNBLOCK
  • Sentinel & cluster pubsub fixes for failover-moved connections and sharded topology recovery
  • Stack modules exposed on pool type and the core client classes are now publicly re-exported

🔥 Breaking Changes

See the v5 → v6 migration guide for migration steps.

  • RESP3 is now the default protocol (#3215)
  • Node.js 20 is now the minimum supported version (#3293)
  • New default values for keepAliveInitialDelay (30s) and commandTimeout (5s) (#3292)

🔒 Security

  • entraid: bump @azure/msal-node to 5.x to drop vulnerable transitive uuid (CVE-2026-41907) (#3269)

🚀 New Features

  • Add CLIENT UNBLOCK command (#3266)
  • Add XNACK command with options (#3238)
  • Add Redis 8.8 array commands (#3285)
  • Add INCREX and INCREXBYFLOAT commands (#3288)
  • Add COUNT aggregator to ZINTER / ZINTERSTORE / ZUNION / ZUNIONSTORE (#3243)
  • Add FPHA option to JSON.SET (#3235)
  • time-series: add multi-aggregation range APIs (#3249)
  • Add duplicate() method to RedisSentinel (#3212)
  • Parse unix:// URLs in parseURL (#3271)
  • Expose RedisClient, RedisCluster, RedisSentinel and pool classes (#3251)
  • Expose stack modules on the pool type (#3267)

🐛 Bug Fixes

  • sentinel: use mapped address when failover moves pubsub connections (#3190)
  • cluster: recover sharded pubsub topology after node reconnects (#3223)
  • client: swallow synchronous EPIPE from writeAfterFIN (#3283)
  • search: allow LOAD * in FT.AGGREGATE (#3241)
  • otel: record client connection errors (#3259)
  • entraid: bump @azure/msal-node to 5.x to drop vulnerable uuid transitive (#3269)
  • Fix wrapper command options handling (#3295)
  • Non-functional typo fixes (#3244)

📚 Documentation

title: [ELUSOC] refactor: standardise error handler middleware response payload structures
body:
# Problem
Database validation errors, duplicate key constraints, and JWT session timeouts return varying payload structures, which complicates error parsing on the frontend.

# Current Behavior
Generic 500 status codes are returned for JWT errors, and Mongo database exceptions leak internal query patterns.

# Why This Improvement Is Needed
Unified, predictable error payloads simplify front-end forms error mapping and hide internal database debug messages in production.

# Proposed Solution
Refactor `error.middleware.js` to intercept Mongoose validation, duplicate entries (11000), and JWT expiration errors, converting them into user-friendly messages.

# Expected Outcome
API error responses return standard JSON structures with correct status codes.

title: [FEATURE] [ELUSOC] security: configure strict CSP headers and request payload body size limits
body:
# Problem
The application does not enforce specific JSON payload limits, exposing it to denial of service attacks through large requests, and leaks backend details via headers.

# Current Behavior
No payload restriction is set on `express.json()`, and the `X-Powered-By` header is exposed by default.

# Why This Improvement Is Needed
Restricting request body limits prevents memory exhaustion from huge POST inputs. Removing server information headers blocks scanner tools from fingerprinting our stack.

# Proposed Solution
Disable `x-powered-by` header, add `limit` boundaries on body-parsing middlewares, and define strict Content Security Policies in Helmet settings.

# Expected Outcome
The server drops large JSON requests (>10MB) early and doesn't reveal technology headers.

title: [FEATURE] [ELUSOC] feat: add centralized environment variables verification utility on startup
body:
# Problem
Currently, the backend configuration module throws a raw error immediately on import if any key is missing, which disrupts test suites and makes local configuration troubleshooting tedious.

# Current Behavior
Importing config crashes runtime if environment variables are not populated, even if the current execution block (like tests) does not need them.

# Why This Improvement Is Needed
A central check running only on server start provides a developer-friendly report outlining exactly what is missing and how to fix it, while keeping utility imports safe.

# Proposed Solution
Extract configuration verification into `envCheck.js`, log pretty formatted warnings on start, and allow configurations to import safely without throwing immediately.

# Expected Outcome
Clean logs on server startup with error details for missing keys, rather than generic import errors.

# Problem
Navbar interactive items lack standard ARIA attributes, violating web accessibility guidelines (WCAG).

# Current Behavior
The theme toggle button has no textual equivalent (`aria-label`), and the language selector dropdown lack standard state bindings.

# Why This Improvement Is Needed
Screen readers cannot identify what the theme toggle button does, and keyboard users cannot easily manage dropdown states.

# Proposed Solution
Add `aria-label`, `aria-pressed`, `aria-haspopup`, and `aria-expanded` attributes to the Navbar elements.

# Expected Outcome
Screen readers cleanly read toggle commands, and dropdown menus advertise open/closed states.

title: [FEATURE] [ELUSOC] fix: resolve duplicate form variable declaration syntax error in image controller
body:
# Problem
The server throws a SyntaxError during execution of the image upload processing route because of a duplicate variable declaration.

# Current Behavior
At lines 104 and 107 in `backend/src/controllers/image.controller.js`, the variable name `form` is declared twice with `const`. Under JavaScript strict mode (and ES module imports), redeclaring a variable in the same scope throws: `SyntaxError: Identifier 'form' has already been declared`.

# Why This Improvement Is Needed
This bug crashes the API route handler when a user attempts to process an uploaded image. It completely blocks background removal and face centering execution.

# Proposed Solution
Remove the duplicate `const form = new FormData();` statement and consolidate the initialization under a single declaration block.

# Expected Outcome
The upload processing route works correctly and relays the request to the Python microservice without syntax errors.

# Additional Notes
Tested locally with a mock file stream to confirm resolution.

title: docs: Add CHANGELOG.md to track project history
body:
## 📋 Issue Description

The project currently does not have a `CHANGELOG.md` file at the root level. A changelog is an essential documentation file that helps contributors and users track what has changed between versions of a project.

## 🔍 What's Missing

There is no `CHANGELOG.md` file in the repository root.

## ✅ Proposed Solution

Add a `CHANGELOG.md` file following the [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/) format, which includes sections like:

- `Added` – for new features
- `Changed` – for changes in existing functionality
- `Deprecated` – for soon-to-be removed features
- `Removed` – for now removed features
- `Fixed` – for any bug fixes
- `Security` – in case of vulnerabilities

## 📁 File Location

`/CHANGELOG.md` (repository root)

## 🙋 Contribution

I would like to work on this if assigned. I'll follow the Keep a Changelog standard and base the initial entries on the existing commit history and README.

---
*This issue was raised as part of improving project documentation and open source contribution standards.*

Pull requests must be manually labeled, making repo organization tedious for maintainers.

# Why This Improvement Is Needed
Automation of issue/PR triage makes repo management scalable.

# Proposed Solution
Set up a GitHub Actions workflow using actions/labeler to auto-assign labels based on modified files.

title: [FEATURE] [ELUSOC] implement schema-based request validation for backend routes
body:
# Problem
Backend routes accept request payloads directly from clients without structured validation.

# Current Behavior
Data from `req.body` or `req.params` is parsed directly, leading to raw JavaScript runtime errors if fields are missing or incorrectly formatted.

# Why This Improvement Is Needed
Robust input validation guarantees backend reliability, prevents unexpected system behaviors, and acts as an important defense-in-depth security layer.

# Proposed Solution
Define explicit request validation schemas using a clean, reusable validation helper to intercept invalid requests early at the routing layer.

# Expected Outcome
Malformed requests get rejected with descriptive 400 Bad Request responses before hitting the controller.

# Additional Notes
This validation targets route schemas for user authentication, image options, and presets.

title: [FEATURE] [ELUSOC] implement rate limiting middleware to prevent backend abuse
body:
# Problem
The backend endpoints currently lack rate limiting, exposing them to brute force, denial of service (DoS), and abuse, particularly the image upload and processing endpoints which are compute-intensive.

# Current Behavior
Any client can call backend API endpoints an infinite number of times without rate limits or throttling.

# Why This Improvement Is Needed
Passport photo processing requires OpenCV/rembg CPU power and server memory. Without rate limiting, malicious users or automated scripts can easily overload the backend server.

# Proposed Solution
Create a reusable express-rate-limit/custom memory-based rate limiting middleware. Integrate it to sensitive routes (upload, process, generate sheet).

# Expected Outcome
APIs return a 429 Too Many Requests status code with a standard JSON error message when the rate limit threshold is exceeded.

# Additional Notes
A custom client IP rate limiter will be built using standard Express request attributes, avoiding external third-party service dependencies if memory usage is kept optimized.
